title: Result[T] single-type-param form: default error type undefined
description: ferrum-lang-types.md uses Result[T] with one type parameter in several places (e.g. 'value.try_into(): Result[T]', 'let p: Result[Percent] = ...'). But the canonical definition is enum Result[T, E] with two type params (confirmed in ferrum-stdlib-core.md). No default error type is defined anywhere in the spec. A caller reading Result[T] cannot know the error type. Either: (a) there is a context-dependent default error type that is not documented, or (b) these are doc errors that should be Result[T, _]. If (a), the default must be specified — what is it? Box[dyn Error]? The Error type from context? If (b), fix the docs. Either way, the current spec leaves callers unable to handle errors from these functions.
